label: Get a credential
description: Help the user understand what the credential is, what it can be used for, receive it via their chosen route, and understand what happens next.
use_when: A service currently issues certificates, letters, or documents.
A decision creates proof that will be needed again by this or other services.
The service completes a decision that confers rights or access.
do_not_use_when: No reusable proof is produced — just a confirmation or receipt.

building_blocks.notes: Required to link the credential to a verified identity.
For notifying the user of credential issuance or delivery.

steps.label: Understand the credential
steps.description: Explain what the credential is, what it can be used for, user responsibility, reuse potential, expiry, and what happens if lost.

steps.label: Confirm eligibility to receive
steps.description: If eligibility has not already been determined, check it. Match to existing records if renewing.

steps.label: Choose how the credential will be provided
steps.description: Present available routes: digital wallet, paper, assisted. User selects preferred method.

steps.label: Receive the credential
steps.description: Issue and make available via the chosen route. Confirm issuance and provide a usable representation.

steps.label: Understand what happens next
steps.description: Confirm issuance, show validity, explain reuse and what to do if lost or expired.

ux_considerations.text: Do not assume the user has a wallet or smartphone. Always provide at least one non-digital route.
Introduce wallets in terms of benefit, not technology.
Explicitly explain credential ownership, reuse, and what happens if lost or expired.

technical_notes.text: Credential issuance must be logged with user reference, credential ID, timestamp, and delivery route.
